  versus disabled SSH remote config propagation, session-level path/args/env/model
  overrides, inactive session shutdown, process kill on shutdown, and swallowed
  kill failures.
- Covered send failure paths for inactive sessions, missing local agents,
  unavailable local agents, thrown agent lookup errors, non-`Error` lookup
  failures, spawn failures, nonzero process exits with and without extracted
  agent session IDs, and response timeout cleanup.
- Covered remote-session behavior where a local agent definition is absent but
  SSH config is enabled, ensuring the spawn command falls back to the agent type
  and SSH config is passed through.
- Covered streamed output parsing for OpenCode text parts, Codex agent messages
  and legacy message events, Claude/result output fallbacks, malformed JSONL
  lines, blank lines, missing text blocks, parser fallback responses, parser
  failure when no structured fallback exists, and extraction of `session_id`.
- Covered callback behavior for `onSending`, `onReceiving`, `onChunk`,
  `onComplete`, `onError`, `onThinkingChunk`, and `onToolExecution`, including
  unrelated session IDs, empty thinking chunks, callback exceptions, listener
  cleanup, and repeated exit cleanup.
- Covered argument building for Claude Code without duplicating existing stream
  args, Codex base args, OpenCode read-only args, OpenCode with no args, and
  unknown agent fallback args.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: Renderer App Constants

Coverage-focused changes:

- Added `src/__tests__/renderer/constants/app.test.ts` for
  `src/renderer/constants/app.ts`.
- Covered `isLikelyConcatenatedToolNames` for concatenated built-in Claude Code
  tool names, whitespace trimming, concatenated MCP tool names, normal prose,
  partial/unknown tool names, too-few tool names, and the core known-tool list
  used by the parser guard.
- Covered `getSlashCommandDescription` for built-in commands with and without a
  leading slash, plugin command formatting, unknown command fallback, and empty
  input fallback.
- Fixed MCP tool detection so `mcp__provider__tool` matches stop before the next
  concatenated `mcp__` token instead of greedily consuming the entire string as a
  single tool.
- Fixed known-tool matching to prefer longer tool names first, so
  `TaskOutputReadWrite` is treated as three concatenated tool names instead of
  incorrectly consuming `Task` and rejecting the remaining `Output...` text.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Markdown Renderer

Coverage-focused changes:

- Extended `src/__tests__/renderer/components/MarkdownRenderer.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`.
- Covered basic markdown, inline code, fenced code with and without languages,
  copy-button behavior, and raw `<pre>` fallback rendering.
- Covered link routing for external HTTP(S) links, `file://` paths,
  `git@host:owner/repo.git` conversion, direct `maestro-file://` links,
  `data-maestro-file` fallbacks, relative links with no shell action, file-tree
  markdown links, project-root absolute path links, and unsafe
  `javascript:` scheme stripping.
- Covered local image behavior for HTTP and `data:image/*` URLs, decoded
  `file://` paths, SSH remote propagation, IPC loading state, module cache reuse,
  same-render cache races, invalid image data, rejected image reads, stale
  resolve/reject cleanup after unmount, width attributes, and missing image
  sources.
- Covered table wrappers/cell styling and raw `<details>` rendering with event
  handler attributes stripped.
- Added a narrow `urlTransform` so ReactMarkdown preserves protocols the
  component already handles (`http`, `https`, `mailto`, `file`,
  `maestro-file`, `git@`, and `data:image/*` for image sources) while continuing
  to collapse unsafe URL schemes such as `javascript:`.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Settings Modal Tabs

- Extended `src/__tests__/renderer/components/SettingsModal.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/Settings/SettingsModal.tsx`.
- Covered the Group Chat settings tab rendering the moderator standing
  instructions control, propagating textarea edits through
  `setModeratorStandingInstructions`, and preserving the visible character
  counter for the controlled value.
- Covered the SSH Hosts settings tab wiring both remote host management and the
  SSH remote ignore settings callbacks for ignore pattern updates and the honor
  `.gitignore` toggle.
- Covered the registered layer-stack Escape handler closing the modal when no
  shortcut recording flow is active.

| File                                                 | Statements     | Branches        | Functions      | Lines          |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | --------------- | -------------- | -------------- |
| `src/renderer/components/Settings/SettingsModal.tsx` | 56.12% (55/98) | 59.72% (86/144) | 66.67% (16/24) | 53.76% (50/93) |

| Branches   | 86.36% (39,379/45,598) | 86.37% (39,386/45,598) |
| Functions  | 91.02% (12,390/13,612) | 91.04% (12,393/13,612) |
| Lines      | 93.76% (55,912/59,627) | 93.77% (55,915/59,627) |

Target-file movement:

- `SettingsModal.tsx` moved from 52.04% statements, 54.86% branches, and
  54.17% functions to 56.12% statements, 59.72% branches, and 66.67%
  functions.
- Remaining uncovered statements are dominated by the dormant LLM connection
  settings flow and `testLLMConnection` path behind the disabled
  `FEATURE_FLAGS.LLM_SETTINGS` flag. That feature flag was not changed, and no
  coverage exclusion was added or widened.
- No production code was changed in this checkpoint.

Validation:

- `npm run test -- src/__tests__/renderer/components/SettingsModal.test.tsx --run --silent`
  passed: 1 file, 111 tests passed.
- `npm run test -- src/__tests__/renderer/components/SettingsModal.test.tsx --run --coverage --silent`
  passed and confirmed `SettingsModal.tsx` at 56.12% statements, 59.72%
  branches, 66.67% functions, and 53.76% lines.
- `npm run test:coverage -- --silent` passed. The project-wide totals and target
  file coverage above came from this run. The run still emitted known suite
  noise from Browserslist, jsdom navigation, duplicate `vs` test mock keys,
  `--localstorage-file`, and expected-error stack traces in existing
  error-boundary/provider tests.

Remaining risk:

- The newly covered paths validate real visible tab behavior and callback
  plumbing, but `SettingsModal.tsx` still has substantial uncovered behavior in
  the disabled LLM settings section. Reaching 100% for that component requires
  either a deliberate feature-flag test seam or explicit approval to exclude
  disabled dormant UI code. Neither change was made silently.
- Settings persistence across app restarts and IPC remains an integration/E2E
  concern for the broader settings workflow.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: App Group Chat Auto Run Bridge

Coverage-focused changes:

- Extended `src/__tests__/renderer/App.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/App.tsx`.
- Covered the app-level Group Chat `!autorun` bridge for recoverable startup
  failures: missing participant session, missing Auto Run folder, configured
  folder with no documents, requested document not found, and failed document
  listing.
- Covered failed reporting back to the moderator when
  `groupChat.reportAutoRunComplete` rejects, including the local `console.error`
  path.
- Covered the successful startup handoff for a targeted Auto Run document:
  `listDocs` receives the configured folder and SSH remote ID, the target
  document is resolved, the group-chat Auto Run registry is populated, and
  `startBatchRun` is invoked with a single-document config.

Remaining risk:

  calls into batch startup. It does not prove full batch execution completion or

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Document Graph Watcher And Preview Navigation

Coverage-focused changes:

- Extended `src/__tests__/renderer/components/DocumentGraph/DocumentGraphView.test.tsx`
  for `src/renderer/components/DocumentGraph/DocumentGraphView.tsx`.

  matching changes invalidate the changed file paths and trigger a debounced
  graph rebuild, while unrelated roots do not invalidate or rebuild.

  back/forward history, opening the current preview file through

## Phase 4/8 Coverage Checkpoint: Group Chat Router Failure And SSH Paths

Coverage-focused changes:

- Extended `src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.test.ts` for
  `src/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.ts`.
- Added a focused Vitest seam around `group-chat-agent.addParticipant` so
  `routeUserMessage` can exercise the recoverable auto-add failure branch
  without changing production behavior.
- Covered user-message routing when an auto-add participant write fails: the
  failed participant is not persisted, the available session remains visible in
  moderator context, and the moderator batch still receives the user request.
- Covered moderator `!autorun` handling when the mentioned participant has no
  Auto Run folder configured.
- Covered participant spawn failure cleanup so pending participant state returns
  to idle.
- Covered moderator synthesis SSH wrapping from moderator remote configuration.

  derived remote ID value, so normal React state changes that keep the same
  remote ID do not re-run that effect. These should not be excluded without
  user approval; if 100% statements are required, they are candidates for a
  small behavior-preserving seam or cleanup rather than artificial tests.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Auto Run Editor, Preview, And Image Race Paths

Coverage-focused changes:

- Extended `src/__tests__/renderer/components/AutoRun.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx`.
- Covered manual save failure behavior: the editor logs the expected failure,
  keeps the dirty content, and leaves the save affordance visible.
- Covered uncontrolled edit/preview mode switching when no external mode
  callback is supplied, including scroll-percentage preservation in both
  directions.
- Covered textarea keyboard delegation when template autocomplete consumes a
  key before Auto Run editor shortcuts.
- Covered command-save behavior for dirty content.
- Covered preview-mode markdown behavior for mermaid blocks, internal
  `maestro-file://` links, external links through `window.maestro.shell`, image
  lightbox opening, highlighted preview search components, preview search close
  focus restoration, and debounced match-count reset when the query changes.
- Covered Auto Run attachment image cache fast paths, cache-populated-after-
  render races, filesystem success/error paths for folder images, absolute
  paths, and relative attachment paths, and stale successful/failed reads after
  preview source changes.
- Covered preview scroll restoration and replacement of pending preview scroll
  notifications.
- Covered focus restoration after document changes in edit and preview modes.
- Covered imperative-handle guards for dirty state, no-op mode switching, and
  no-op save when content is not dirty.
- Covered reset-tasks guard behavior when the selected document has no backing
  Auto Run folder.
- No production code was changed.

- Mocked the shared activity bus locally so activity debounce, unsubscribe, and
  restart behavior can be tested deterministically.
- Covered hidden-document polling skips with `pauseWhenHidden` enabled.
- Covered active-session polling through `window.maestro.git.info`,
  `gitService.getStatus`, and `gitService.getNumstat`, including terminal
  `shellCwd`, per-session SSH remote fallback, branch/remote/ahead/behind
  mapping, porcelain status trimming, modification detection, numstat merging,
  total addition/deletion aggregation, and missing-numstat defaults.
- Covered per-session git fetch failures so a broken session is dropped without
  losing successful session status data.
- Covered same-size status map replacement when the git session ID changes.
- Covered polling interval stop on inactivity, continued polling while active,
  activity debounce timer replacement, activity restart after inactivity,
  pending debounce cleanup on unmount, visibility-change stop/start behavior,
  and interval restart when git session count crosses the dynamic scaling
  threshold.
- No production code was changed.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: CLI Batch Processor Service

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/cli/services/batch-processor.test.ts` for
  `src/cli/services/batch-processor.ts`.
- Covered default Auto Run prompt fallback for blank playbook prompts.
- Covered usage-stat accumulation when optional token/cost fields are missing.
- Covered task failure fallback when an agent returns no error text.
- Covered synopsis fallback when the synopsis agent returns no response.
- Replaced the skipped max-loop test with a bounded two-loop scenario that
  proves loop completion, max-loop exit, loop-summary history, final-loop
  history, total Auto Run history, usage aggregation, and pluralized summaries.
- Covered mixed reset and non-reset documents through a real loop continuation
  without debug/history output.
- Tightened the document-template expansion test so it verifies write-back of
  expanded `{{CWD}}` content.
- Covered blank git branch handling and quiet loop exits for completed
  non-reset docs, all-reset docs, and the no-work safety check.
- No production code was changed.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: Renderer Tab Helpers Unified Edge Cases

## Phase 3/8 Coverage Checkpoint: WebSocket Hook Completion

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/web/hooks/useWebSocket.test.ts` for
  `src/web/hooks/useWebSocket.ts`.
- Covered disconnect with no active socket, stale open/error events from
  replaced connections, empty `session_output` payload logging when no output
  handler is registered, automatic ping intervals when the socket becomes
  closed before the timer fires, and reconnect-limit failure reporting.
- Removed the unreachable mount-id cleanup guard from `useWebSocket.ts`. The
  effect has an empty dependency array, and React's dev StrictMode cleanup for
  the first effect run happens before the second setup increments the ref, so
  the guarded early return was not reachable through the hook lifecycle.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: File Preview

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/FilePreview.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/FilePreview.tsx`.
- Covered trailing-empty extensions, C1 control-character binary detection,
  markdown image cache expiry, URL-decoded local image paths, file-tree image
  fallback outside the cwd, unsupported markdown link protocols, full-node
  markdown and code search matches, disabled navigation popups, repeated scroll
  restoration for the same file, edit-to-preview scroll synchronization,
  tab-mode escape/click-outside no-op behavior, Ctrl shortcut variants, search
  input fall-through keys, selection-direction anchors, and textarea page
  movement boundaries.

  synchronization by retaining the last mounted textarea element long enough to
  transfer its scroll percentage.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Settings Modal LLM Feature-Flag Seam

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/SettingsModal.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/Settings/SettingsModal.tsx`.
- Added a minimal, default-off `featureFlags.llmSettings` prop seam so the
  dormant LLM settings panel remains disabled in production by default while
  being reachable in tests.
- Extracted the LLM connection check into `testSettingsLlmConnection`, a small
  pure helper used by the modal. This preserves modal behavior while making
  provider network success, provider API failures, malformed provider responses,
  and missing API-key guards independently testable.
- Covered LLM panel tab rendering, provider selection, model slug edits, API key
  edits, OpenRouter success/API error/invalid response, Anthropic
  success/API error/invalid response, Ollama success/API error/invalid response,
  and missing API-key guards that normally sit behind a disabled button.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Execution Process Failures And Queue Drain

  agent execution.

  agent-session capture, reasoning-token accumulation, usage-stat aggregation,
  and local `console.warn` handling when query-stat recording fails.

  user log, missing target-tab fallback, empty-tab idle transitions,
  non-target-session preservation, and non-worktree queue-drain polling before
  batch resolution.

  exceptions, failed synopsis process spawns, listener cleanup, main-session SSH
  fallback, explicit session SSH override, custom session config propagation,
  unrelated synopsis listener isolation, and empty args fallback.
- Covered synopsis cancellation no-ops, successful kill, failed kill warning,
  tracking cleanup, and multiple pending synopsis sessions for one Maestro
  session.
- No production code changed and no coverage exclusion was added or widened.

  100%, prefer a tiny behavior-preserving cleanup of the unused prop over adding
  a brittle test-only seam.
- Remaining branch gaps are branch-only fallbacks in text wrapping, optional
  wand/social icon drawing, and high-level badge history coloring. They are not
  excluded.
- Full coverage still prints pre-existing expected-error noise from unrelated
  error-boundary/context tests. This checkpoint locally spies on expected
  console errors and removes one jsdom navigation warning by spying on the
  download anchor click path.
- The next largest missed-statement targets after this checkpoint are
  `App.tsx` (36), `PlaygroundPanel.tsx` (22),
  `useAutoRunImageHandling.ts` (22), `CommandInputBar.tsx` (22),

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Sessions Modal Provider, Layer, And Pagination Paths

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/AgentSessionsModal.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/AgentSessionsModal.tsx`.
- Covered generic provider origin loading and generic starred-session
  persistence for non-Claude agents.
- Covered updated layer Escape handling in list view and defensive layer
  registration behavior when no layer id is returned.
- Covered the guard where an active agent loses `cwd` before a provider session
  is opened.
- Covered scroll-triggered message pagination and no-cursor session pagination.
- Reset shared Maestro window mocks per test so negative assertions are not
  polluted by previous test calls.
- Made two minimal behavior-preserving production cleanups: the initial layer
  Escape handler now performs only its reachable list-view close behavior while
  current session-view Escape behavior remains in the already-tested
  `updateLayerHandler`, and scroll handlers now use the scroll event's
  `currentTarget` instead of mutable refs.
- No coverage exclusion was added or widened.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Config Model And Environment Edits

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded
  `src/__tests__/renderer/components/shared/AgentConfigPanel.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/shared/AgentConfigPanel.tsx`.
- Covered model filtering cleanup when a user types a filter and clicks outside
  without selecting a model, proving the committed model is preserved.
- Covered the delayed model blur guard after selecting a dropdown item so the
  selection is not overwritten by the input blur timer.
- Covered abandoning an empty model filter on blur, which restores the committed
  value and persists the existing model.
- Covered non-model text config fields with parent rerender before blur, matching
  how controlled consumers commit direct text edits.
- Covered stale environment-variable display cleanup when keys are removed and a
  different key is rendered.
- Covered click propagation guards for path, custom args, env key/value, model,
  numeric config, and select config fields so parent overlays do not receive
  field clicks.
- No production code changed and no coverage exclusion was added or widened.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Tab Switcher Search, Hotkeys, And Sync Noise

Coverage-focused changes:

- Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/TabSwitcherModal.test.tsx` for
  `src/renderer/components/TabSwitcherModal.tsx`.
- Covered non-Claude named-tab synchronization through provider session storage,
  including local warning behavior when provider name persistence fails.
- Covered reverse `Shift+Tab` mode cycling across Open Tabs, Starred, and All
  Named modes, plus returning to Open Tabs through the mode pill.
- Covered closed named-session fuzzy search by session ID.
- Covered scroll-position-aware number hotkeys so visible-number shortcuts map
  to the scrolled item window rather than always selecting from index zero.
- Reduced `TabSwitcherModal.test.tsx` signal noise by making the default
  named-session load stay pending in tests that do not assert named-session
  loading. Tests that need named sessions still override the mock and wait for
  the load explicitly.
- No production code changed and no coverage exclusion was added or widened.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Symphony Hook Registry, Issue Count, And Contribution Failures

Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/symphony/useSymphony.test.ts` for
`src/renderer/hooks/symphony/useSymphony.ts`.

Added coverage for:

- Registry responses that omit optional cache fields and thrown non-`Error`
  registry failures.
- Symphony state responses that omit state and state-fetch exceptions that log
  without blocking registry loading.
- Unknown category sorting, featured repository ordering when featured repos are
  not already first, missing issue responses, and thrown issue fetches.
- Issue-count responses without counts, failed issue-count fetches, and stale
  issue-count cleanup when a refreshed registry has no active repositories.
- Periodic auto-sync with active contributions, no-active skip behavior, and
  auto-sync failure logging.
- Clone/start fallback errors, thrown start-contribution failures, cancel
  fallback/exception paths, and finalize fallback/exception paths.

Production behavior change:

- `src/renderer/hooks/symphony/useSymphony.ts` now always routes repository list
  changes through `fetchIssueCounts(repositories)`. This preserves the existing
  non-empty behavior and also clears stale issue counts when the active
  repository list becomes empty.

  structurally unreachable branches where the listener parser would need
  `isFromAi === true` while no AI tab id was parsed from the session id. Real
  string session ids either match `session-ai-tab` and provide a tab id or are
  treated as non-AI. Covering those branches would require synthetic non-string
  inputs or monkey-patching `String.prototype.match`, which would be metric
  padding rather than product behavior.
- The hook still has many branch-only gaps around toast/session summary
  fallbacks and defensive optional fields. Those are not approved exclusions.
- The next largest missed-statement targets after this checkpoint are

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: App Shell Auto-Send And Auto Run Guards

Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/App.test.tsx` for `src/renderer/App.tsx`,
and fixed the delayed auto-send path in `src/renderer/App.tsx` so it sends the
settled active tab input when the timeout fires.

Added App shell coverage for:

- Successful auto-send of pending merged context when the activated target
  remains active.
- Stale-target cancellation when the original auto-send tab is removed before
  the delayed send fires.
- Auto Run setup modal routing when switching to the Auto Run tab without a
  configured folder.

Production behavior fixed:

- The delayed auto-send effect now calls `processInput(currentTab.inputValue)`
  instead of `processInput()`. This preserves the prompt that was already stored
  on the target tab when the timeout fires, including pending merged context,
  and avoids depending on local input context state catching up first.

Remaining risk:

- The remaining uncovered `AgentSessionsBrowser.tsx` statements are Escape-key
  handlers for the layer registration callback, detail/list keydown branches,
  the detail rename field, and the search input. In the current app wiring,
  `LayerStackProvider` handles Escape during the window capture phase and stops
  propagation before those child `onKeyDown` handlers can run. Covering them
  directly would require either a behavior-preserving event-model change or
  deleting unreachable handler branches; neither was done silently in this
  checkpoint.
- `AgentSessionsBrowser.tsx` still has branch gaps around fallback display
  states, search mode variants, and detail rendering. Those should be covered
  with user-visible list/detail/search behavior, not handler pokes.
- The next largest missed-statement targets after this checkpoint are

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Listener Legacy AI Events

Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/useAgentListeners.test.ts` and made a
small behavior-preserving parser alignment in
`src/renderer/hooks/agent/useAgentListeners.ts`.

Added Agent Listener coverage for:

- Legacy AI stdout events in the documented `{sessionId}-ai` format, routed to
  the current write-mode tab when no explicit tab id is present.
- Legacy AI stdout events that have no target AI tab, including the local
  `console.error` assertion and early return without appending a log.
- Legacy AI process exits for errored sessions, preserving the agent error while
  clearing busy tab state.
- Legacy AI process exits for successful sessions, clearing every busy AI tab
  when no explicit tab id is present.

The source change replaces duplicate regex-only parsing in `onData` and
`onExit` with the existing `parseSessionId` utility for AI-tab and legacy-AI
process IDs, while preserving the existing terminal and broad batch early-return
behavior.

## Phase 5 Coverage Checkpoint: Group Chat Storage File-Lock Recovery

Expanded `src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-storage.test.ts` for
`src/main/group-chat/group-chat-storage.ts`.

Added Group Chat Storage coverage for:

- Atomic metadata write retry after transient `EPERM` rename failures.
- Atomic metadata write propagation for non-retryable `EACCES` rename failures,
  including verification that the previously persisted metadata remains intact.
- Group chat deletion retry after transient locked-directory `ENOTEMPTY`
  failures.
- Group chat deletion propagation for non-retryable `EACCES` removal failures,
  including verification that the chat remains loadable.
- Local assertion for the expected malformed-history `console.warn`, removing
  the noisy warning from the focused test output without hiding the behavior.

The tests use a local `fs/promises` mock whose `rename` and `rm` functions call
the real filesystem implementation by default, with one-call overrides for the
fault-injection cases. The existing temporary-directory harness still exercises
real file creation, metadata reads, JSONL history, and recursive cleanup.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Sessions Browser Layer Escape Handler

Updated `src/renderer/components/AgentSessionsBrowser.tsx` and validated
`src/__tests__/renderer/components/AgentSessionsBrowser.test.tsx`.

Changed Agent Sessions Browser coverage/behavior by:

- Replacing duplicate layer Escape callbacks with a shared
  `handleLayerEscape` callback used by both initial registration and handler
  updates.
- Preserving existing user-visible behavior: Escape in the list closes the
  browser through the layer stack, and Escape in detail view returns to the
  list.

  currently bypassed by the real `LayerStackProvider` window-capture Escape
  handler.

  not be done silently as a coverage-only change.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Maestro Wizard Step Transition Timers

Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/Wizard/MaestroWizard.test.tsx` and
made a narrow production fix in
`src/renderer/components/Wizard/MaestroWizard.tsx`.

Added Maestro Wizard coverage for:

- Opening the wizard from a closed state with a restored step, verifying the
  displayed step synchronizes on open and the screen reader announcement matches
  the restored step.
- In-session step changes, verifying the content enters the fade-out state,
  advances to the next screen after the fade timers, and returns to the entering
  state.
- Interrupted step transitions, verifying pending fade timers are cleaned up
  safely on unmount.

Production behavior fixed:

- Restored-step synchronization now runs only when the wizard opens, instead of
  on every current-step change. This preserves the intended fade transition for
  normal in-session navigation.
- The fade-out effect now reads `isTransitioning` through a ref so setting
  `isTransitioning` to `true` does not re-run the effect and clear its own
  pending fade-out timer.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Auto Run Expanded Dirty Close and Escape

Expanded `src/__tests__/renderer/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.test.tsx` and
made a narrow production cleanup in
`src/renderer/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx`.

Added Auto Run Expanded modal coverage for:

- Embedded Auto Run state changes, verifying the expanded modal preserves the
  parent panel's mode while forwarding cursor and scroll state.
- Dirty close handling, verifying close attempts show the Unsaved Changes
  confirmation instead of closing.
- Discard and cancel paths from the Unsaved Changes confirmation, verifying
  discard reverts then closes while cancel leaves the expanded modal open.
- Layer-stack Escape routing, verifying Escape uses the current dirty-state close
  handler.
- Async save-click tests now wrap the click in `act`, removing the warning noise
  that previously appeared in this file.

Production behavior kept equivalent while simplifying the Escape handler:

- The layer Escape handler is now a single stable callback that reads
  `handleCloseRef.current`, so both the initially registered handler and updated
  handler use the same current dirty-state close logic.
- The close ref now initializes to `onClose` instead of a no-op fallback.

| File                                           | Statements       | Branches         | Functions         | Lines             |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- | ----------------- |

Validation:

- `npx vitest run src/__tests__/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.test.tsx --coverage --coverage.include=src/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx`
  passed: 1 file and 102 tests passed.

Remaining uncovered statements:

- Create-side model loading early return when a caller requests models for an
  agent without model-selection support.
- Create-side `handleCreate` defensive returns for blank names and invalid
  validation results.
- Edit-side copy-session guard when no session exists.
- Edit-side `handleSave` defensive returns for missing session, blank names,
  and invalid validation results.
- Edit-side model refresh early return when the active agent lacks
  model-selection support.

Assessment:

- These paths are currently guarded by the rendered UI before the callbacks can
  run: disabled Create/Save buttons, keyboard shortcuts gated by `isFormValid`,
  `EditAgentModal` returning `null` without a session, and
  `AgentConfigPanel` hiding model-refresh controls for agents without
  `supportsModelSelection`.
- Covering them meaningfully would require a small test seam that exposes the
  internal callbacks or a product change that allows invoking those handlers
  from the rendered tree. No such seam was introduced in this checkpoint, and
  no coverage exclusion was added or widened.
- Existing tests already cover the user-visible invalid form states,
  duplicate-name validation, keyboard shortcut gating, SSH remote validation,
  copy success/failure behavior, provider switching, model refresh success and
  failure, config persistence failures, and create/edit submission payloads.

## Phase 4/8 Coverage Assessment: Group Chat Router Defensive Guards

Rechecked `src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.test.ts` against
`src/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.ts` before adding more tests.

Focused coverage:

| File                                       | Statements       | Branches         | Functions       | Lines            |
| ------------------------------------------ | ---------------- | ---------------- | --------------- | ---------------- |
| `src/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.ts` | 98.88% (623/630) | 86.99% (321/369) | 100.00% (56/56) | 98.85% (603/610) |

Validation:

- `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.test.ts --coverage --coverage.include=src/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.ts --coverage.reportsDirectory=/tmp/maestro-group-chat-router-coverage`
  passed: 1 file and 105 tests passed.
- The focused run emitted substantial existing Group Chat debug stdout/stderr.
  This is a remaining Phase 2 test-signal cleanup target; it was not silenced
  globally in this checkpoint.

Remaining uncovered statements:

- `routeUserMessage` logs the no-session-id-prefix warning after
  `isModeratorActive(groupChatId)` returns true. With the current exported
  `group-chat-moderator` API, moderator activity and moderator session id come
  from the same internal map, so normal setup either has both values or neither.
- `routeModeratorResponse` has a defensive participant-not-found skip inside
  the participant spawn loop. The loop is populated from `extractMentions`
  against the same `updatedChat.participants` object that the loop later
  searches, so this branch requires an internal mutation between mention
  extraction and iteration.
- `spawnModeratorSynthesis` has the same active-moderator-without-session-id
  guard as `routeUserMessage`.

Assessment:

- These remaining statements are defensive consistency guards rather than
  reachable behavior through the public router and moderator APIs. Covering them
  meaningfully would require a narrow test seam for inconsistent moderator
  session state or mutable participant snapshots.
- No production seam was introduced in this checkpoint, and no coverage
  exclusion was added or widened.
- Existing tests already cover group chat message routing, moderator and
  participant spawning, unavailable agents, missing chats, no process manager
  paths, auto-add failures, Auto Run directives, SSH wrapping for moderator,
  participant, synthesis, and recovery paths, Windows shell handling, storage
  failures, timeout cleanup, and synthesis lifecycle cleanup.

  catch block after `safeClipboardWriteImage` already catches failures, copy
  markdown with no filename, and prompt-delete guard after callers already gate
  by local image plus `onDelete`.
- Covering those paths meaningfully would require exposing internal callbacks or
  changing the clipboard utility contract to throw. No such seam was introduced
  in this checkpoint.

## Phase 6 Coverage Assessment: File Explorer Panel Guard Paths

Rechecked `src/__tests__/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.test.tsx`
against `src/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.tsx` before adding more
tests.

Focused coverage:

| File                                            | Statements       | Branches         | Functions      | Lines            |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | ---------------- | -------------- | ---------------- |
| `src/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.tsx` | 97.91% (328/335) | 88.23% (285/323) | 98.80% (83/84) | 98.74% (315/319) |

Validation:

- `npx vitest run src/__tests__/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.test.tsx --coverage --coverage.include=src/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.tsx --coverage.reportsDirectory=/tmp/maestro-file-explorer-panel-coverage`
  passed: 1 file and 155 tests passed.

Remaining uncovered statements:

- Auto-refresh effect clearing `autoRefreshTimerRef.current` at the top of the
  effect. In normal React lifecycle, the prior effect cleanup clears the timer
  before the next interval effect runs, and unmount/interval-change cleanup is
  already covered.
- `handleRename` blank-name and same-name guards. The rendered rename modal
  disables confirmation and form submission for blank or unchanged names before
  the callback can run.
- `handleDelete` missing-delete-modal guard. The delete confirmation modal only
  renders when `deleteModal` exists, so the confirm callback is not reachable
  without an active modal.
- `filterHiddenFiles` undefined-node guard. The memoized caller passes
  `filteredFileTree || []`, and recursive calls are gated by `node.children`.

Assessment:

- These are defensive consistency guards behind UI disabling, modal render
  gates, React effect cleanup, or normalized caller inputs. Existing tests cover
  the user-visible behavior: hidden-file filtering, filter layer Escape,
  refresh and auto-refresh success/failure, retry countdowns, context menu
  actions, rename validation and filesystem failure, folder and file deletion,
  SSH-aware operations, local stats updates, and virtualized tree rendering.
- Covering the remaining statements meaningfully would require exposing
  internal callbacks or adding a test-only seam for invalid internal states. No
  such seam was introduced in this checkpoint, and no coverage exclusion was
  added or widened.

## Phase 6 Coverage Assessment: Send To Agent Modal Status And Disabled Send Guards

Rechecked `src/__tests__/renderer/components/SendToAgentModal.test.tsx` against
`src/renderer/components/SendToAgentModal.tsx` before adding more tests.

Focused coverage:

| File                                           | Statements       | Branches         | Functions      | Lines            |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | ---------------- | -------------- | ---------------- |
| `src/renderer/components/SendToAgentModal.tsx` | 96.19% (177/184) | 89.54% (137/153) | 97.95% (48/49) | 96.36% (159/165) |

Validation:

- `npx vitest run src/__tests__/renderer/components/SendToAgentModal.test.tsx --coverage --coverage.include=src/renderer/components/SendToAgentModal.tsx --coverage.reportsDirectory=/tmp/maestro-send-to-agent-modal-coverage`
  passed: 1 file, 20 tests passed, and 25 legacy tests skipped.

Remaining uncovered statements:

- `getStatusLabel('current')` and `getStatusColor('current')`, plus the
  fallback/default cases. The component filters the source session out before it
  maps sessions to status options, so `current` is not reachable through the
  rendered list despite the legacy status helper still supporting it.
- The `session.id === sourceSession.id` branch inside the status mapper. This is
  guarded by the earlier source-session filter.
- The layer-stack `onEscape` inline callback is behaviorally covered by the
  Escape test, but V8 still reports the inline statement as uncovered.
- `handleSend` with no selected session. The Send button is disabled and
  keyboard handling selects the highlighted session before sending, so this
  guard is not reachable through normal UI.

Assessment:

- Existing active tests cover session filtering, search, empty states, fallback
  names, token estimates, default and disabled clean-context options, send
  success/failure, sending state, layer-stack Escape behavior, Enter/numeric
  selection, busy status rendering, and accessibility basics.
- The 25 skipped legacy tests are remaining test-quality debt from the old
  agent-selection interface. They should be deleted or rewritten in a future
  cleanup pass, but not as a coverage shortcut in this checkpoint.
- No production seam was introduced, and no coverage exclusion was added or
  widened.

  coverage requires them, the clean option is a small production cleanup of the
  stale `current` status path or an explicitly approved narrow exclusion.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Documents Panel Refresh And Drag Guards

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/DocumentsPanel.test.tsx`.
- `src/renderer/components/DocumentsPanel.tsx`.

Added coverage for:

- Refresh status messages clearing after their 3-second timeout.
- Drag-over events before a drag starts, proving they do not create a drop
  indicator.
- Drag-leave behavior during an active drag, proving the drop indicator remains
  visible until drop or drag-end cleanup.

Production fix:

- Split the document-selector refresh-message effect into separate "compute the
  message" and "clear the current message" effects. The previous effect set
  `prevDocCount` and returned a timeout cleanup in the same pass, so the state
  update immediately cleaned up the 3-second timeout before it could clear the
  message.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Weekday Comparison Chart Duration States

## Recommended Coverage Policy

100% line coverage should not be treated as sufficient proof of comprehensive tests. The target should include:

- 100% coverage for pure utilities, IPC service wrappers, parser logic, and storage transformation helpers.
- Explicit integration tests for IPC handlers, storage adapters, SSH wrapping, and process spawning.
- E2E tests for critical app workflows: agent creation, prompt send/interrupt, Auto Run, file preview, settings persistence, remote sessions, and recovery/error states.
- Mutation or fault-injection checks for high-risk logic where simple coverage can pass without proving behavior.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: CLI JSON-Line Spawner Guards

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/cli/services/agent-spawner.test.ts`.

  resolved custom executable path instead of falling back to a default binary.

  with assertions that the warning was emitted, so those tests no longer write
  expected errors to stderr.
- JSON-line agent output that includes blank lines and parser-null events,
  proving the spawner skips non-events without losing valid response and usage
  data.
- Misconfigured JSON-line-capable agent definitions that do not have a parser,
  proving configuration drift fails loudly instead of silently producing a
  misleading result.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Configuration Async Failures

## Residual Gap Triage: Defensive And Unreachable Renderer Paths

Reviewed after the Git Status Widget checkpoint using
`coverage/coverage-final.json` from the passing `npm run test:coverage` run.
This triage did not change coverage; it records why several tempting gaps were
not converted into brittle tests.

Reviewed gaps:

- `src/renderer/components/LogViewer.tsx`: missed lines 228-230 are in the
  mount-time registered escape handler, whose closure captures the initial
  `searchOpen=false`. The live updated layer handler covers the search-open
  behavior. Reaching the initial branch would require a component seam or a
  React internals mock.
- `src/web/mobile/CommandInputBar.tsx`: missed lines 369-371 are the terminal
  branch of the shared textarea key handler. Terminal mode currently renders a
  single-line input with its own Enter handler, while shared textareas are AI
  mode only.
- `src/main/power-manager.ts`: missed lines 171-172 and 194-195 are private
  defensive guards. Public methods call `startBlocking` only when no blocker is
  active and `stopBlocking` only when a blocker exists.
- `src/renderer/components/SaveMarkdownModal.tsx`: missed empty-folder and
  empty-filename save guards are behind a disabled Save button and an Enter
  shortcut guard.
- `src/renderer/components/FileExplorerPanel.tsx`: remaining focused gaps include
  auto-refresh timer replacement cleanup, unchanged rename, null tree filtering,
  and delete-modal in-flight close protection. The unchanged rename and empty
  branch paths are behind disabled controls or caller guards; the in-flight
  delete path is meaningful but needs a better seam because pending delete
  promises interact poorly with the current test event/timer harness.
- `src/renderer/components/ExecutionQueueIndicator.tsx`: missed zero-tab resize
  branch requires a rendered queue with zero tab names, but non-empty queues
  always provide a real or fallback `Unknown` tab name, and empty queues render
  `null`.
- `src/renderer/components/AICommandsPanel.tsx`,
  `OpenSpecCommandsPanel.tsx`, and `SpecKitCommandsPanel.tsx`: missed save,
  create, and delete guards are behind disabled or hidden controls in the
  rendered UI.
- `src/web/mobile/ResponseViewer.tsx`: missed zoom snap-back is unreachable
  because pinch scale clamps at `MIN_ZOOM = 1`; the later display-response null
  guard is dominated by the prior open/response null return.
- `src/renderer/hooks/keyboard/useListNavigation.ts`: missed page-navigation
  zero-length guards are inside internal callbacks that are not returned; the
  public `handleKeyDown` returns before calling them when `listLength === 0`.
- `src/renderer/components/CsvTableRenderer.tsx`: missed empty numeric helper
  and empty-query highlight helper paths are avoided by callers that filter
  empty cells and call highlighting only for non-empty queries.
- `src/web/mobile/MessageHistory.tsx`: missed `getTruncatedText` fallthrough is
  unreachable because the helper is only called after `shouldTruncate` is true;
  the scroll-container null branch is a defensive ref guard.
- `src/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.ts`: missed stale-participant guard
  would require an inconsistent group-chat state where a mention remains after
  the participant has disappeared.
- `src/renderer/hooks/tabs/useTabHandlers.ts`: missed `createTab` null-result
  guards require the tab factory to reject an otherwise valid active session.

Decision:

- No exclusions were added and no approval has been requested yet.
- These items should be treated as candidates for either documented exclusions
  or small behavior-preserving production cleanup if the campaign decides to
  chase the final coverage fraction. They should not be covered with import-only
  tests or React internals mocks.

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/Wizard/screens/AgentSelectionScreen.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added remote detection coverage for mixed results where at least one remote
  agent is available, proving the screen does not show the connection-failure
  banner unless every visible remote result is failed or unavailable.
- Added config-panel coverage for a selected SSH remote, including model load
  and model refresh calls with the selected remote id.
- Added config fallback coverage for `getConfig()` returning `null`, proving
  the config panel uses empty defaults rather than crashing.
- Added enabled-but-unselected SSH remote context coverage from wizard state,
  proving remote selects render with an empty value and model calls use
  `undefined` for the remote id.

  the generic `Unknown connection error` message while still logging the thrown
  value.
- Added placeholder config-panel coverage for supported tiles missing from
  detection results, preserving the ability to configure a custom path for an
  otherwise unavailable supported agent.
- Added customize-button keyboard guard coverage: unrelated keys do not open the
  panel, while Space does.
- Added empty custom-path blur coverage, proving the detector receives `null`
  for blank paths before refreshing detection.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: Batch Processor Error Controls And Worktree Loop Details

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/useBatchProcessor.test.ts`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added public error-control coverage for missing batch state, proving
  `pauseBatchOnError`, duplicate pause requests, `skipCurrentDocument`, and
  `resumeAfterError` do not broadcast stale Auto Run state when no batch is
  active.
- Added abort coverage for a missing batch without a pending error resolution,
  proving the abort path can be called defensively and still clears remote Auto
  Run state.
- Added worktree loop-start coverage for an empty custom prompt, proving the
  worktree audit history includes loop-mode/max-loop details and no empty prompt
  leaks into broadcast payloads.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Sessions Browser Escape Cleanup

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/AgentSessionsBrowser.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/AgentSessionsBrowser.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Removed stale local Escape handling from `AgentSessionsBrowser` key handlers.
  `LayerStackProvider` captures Escape globally before local component handlers
  run, so the local branches were unreachable in normal UI behavior.
- The search input now delegates directly to `handleKeyDown` instead of carrying
  an unreachable local Escape branch.

  cancellation branch. Enter and blur rename behavior are preserved.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: Agent Listener onData Race Branches

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/useAgentListeners.test.ts`.

## Phase 3 Coverage Checkpoint: Batch Processor Debounced Progress Branches

- `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/batch/useBatchProcessor.debounce.test.ts`.

Coverage added or clarified:

  preserves progress fields and broadcasts the current Auto Run state without
  treating missing `documents` as a nonzero document count.

- `src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-router.test.ts`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Removed an unreachable participant-not-found branch after `extractMentions`.
  That helper only returns names from the same reloaded participant list used by
  the spawn loop, so the subsequent exact participant lookup is an invariant.
- Removed redundant exact-name session matching fallbacks where `mentionMatches`
  already covers exact matches.
- Added failure-path coverage for a mentioned participant whose agent detector
  returns no agent config, verifying the router does not spawn a process and
  clears pending participant state.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Activity Graph Time And Bucket Edges

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/History/ActivityGraph.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/History/ActivityGraph.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added coverage for unknown lookback values falling back to the default
  `24 hours` graph configuration.
- Added short-window tooltip coverage for noon/PM formatting and centered
  tooltip placement.
- Added historical hover tooltip coverage so the reference-time label appears
  in the tooltip, not only the graph title.
- Added coverage for empty all-time windows and zero-width all-time windows
  where all entries share the reference timestamp.
- Added coverage for out-of-window entries and malformed runtime entry types,
  proving they do not inflate AUTO or USER counts.
- Removed the unreachable sub-minute `formatReferenceTime()` branch because the
  formatter is only called when a reference time is already more than one minute
  old.
- Guarded all-time bucket sizing with `Math.max(1, ...)` and removed the
  redundant post-clamp bucket-index guard so same-timestamp all-time entries are
  counted deterministically instead of producing a `NaN` bucket index.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Session Pagination Origins And Auto-Load Guards

Updated:

- `src/__tests__/renderer/hooks/useSessionPagination.test.ts`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added Claude origin coverage for malformed non-object, non-string origin
  values, proving they do not overwrite session origin data.
- Added generic provider origin coverage for unstarred imported sessions,
  proving they merge session names and origins without adding false positives to
  the starred-session set.
- Added scroll coverage below the 70% pagination threshold before asserting the
  above-threshold load-more path.
- Strengthened the duplicate-page auto-load test with a deferred second page so
  the hook observes `isLoadingMoreSessions` transitioning back to `false` while
  the loaded session count is unchanged, covering the guard that prevents a
  repeated background-load loop.

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/InlineWizard/StreamingDocumentPreview.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/InlineWizard/InlineWizardDocumentViews.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added scroll behavior coverage for near-bottom scrolls, manual scroll-up
  detection, content updates while auto-scroll is paused, filename-change reset,
  and the resume auto-scroll control.
- Added filename fallback coverage for missing filenames and rerendering from a
  concrete filename back to the generated fallback label.
- Added markdown-link behavior coverage proving relative links are not opened
  through `shell.openExternal`, while the existing external-link test continues
  to prove `https` links are opened.
- Changed scroll handling to read dimensions from the scroll event target,
  removing the unreachable ref-null branch from the private scroll handler.
- Removed the duplicate URL-scheme guard inside the component-level external
  link callback, relying on `createMarkdownComponents` as the shared link
  classifier before invoking the callback.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Pull To Refresh Color And Spinner Branches

Updated:

- `src/web/components/PullToRefresh.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/web/components/PullToRefresh.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

  component is only rendered while `isRefreshing` is true, and the non-spinning
  refresh-icon path was not reachable through `PullToRefreshIndicator`.

  stripped before composing the indicator background color.

  background color falls back to black rather than emitting invalid RGB values.

## Phase 3/8 Coverage Checkpoint: Group Chat Log Malformed Lines And Image Guard

Updated:

- `src/main/group-chat/group-chat-log.ts`.
- `src/__tests__/main/group-chat/group-chat-log.test.ts`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added malformed-line coverage proving `readLog()` skips entries with too few
  unescaped separators while still parsing valid neighboring messages.

  controlled UUID mock, proving `saveImage()` rejects a resolved path outside

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Mobile App Connection And Interrupt Cleanup

Updated:

- `src/web/mobile/App.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/web/mobile/App.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Removed the redundant empty-text branch in `getFirstLineOfResponse`. The
  response-complete flow only calls the formatter when `lastResponse.text` is
  truthy, and the existing all-skipped-markdown path still returns
  `Response completed`.
- Simplified initial connection scheduling by relying on timeout clearing and
  load-listener removal during effect cleanup instead of carrying cancelled
  branches that cannot run after cleanup.
- Passed the WebSocket `send` callback directly to the offline queue send
  adapter, matching the hook's documented usage and removing an unnecessary
  local ref fallback.
- Refactored draft clearing to receive the active session ID after
  `handleCommandSubmit` has already verified that a session is active.
- Refactored interrupt handling to receive the active session ID from the render
  site. The interrupt callback is only exposed when an active session exists.
- Added a regression test proving the mobile app does not expose the interrupt
  control when no session is active.

  non-interactive status cards while available and blocked issues still receive
  explicit selection handlers.

  dropdown only renders from the selected-issue view, and document navigation
  already reads from the selected issue in the same view state.

  behavior depends on the selected document path and external/local flag, not on
  the currently selected repository object.

  contribution preflight. The start action is only exposed from the selected
  repository and issue UI and only opens the GitHub CLI/build-warning preflight.

  creation. The agent dialog is configured with the repository and issue being
  started, and creation now relies on that explicit config payload.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Log Viewer Guard Cleanup

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/LogViewer.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Removed the stale search branch from the initially registered layer Escape
  handler. That handler closes over the initial `searchOpen === false` state;
  search-specific Escape behavior is already owned by the updated layer handler.
- Removed unreachable `layerIdRef.current` guards around unregister/update
  calls. `registerLayer` returns a required string, and React runs the
  registration effect before the update effect in this component.
- Removed mounted-container guards from Alt+Arrow page scrolling and timeline
  segment scrolling. The scroll container is rendered while the LogViewer is
  mounted and interactive.
- Removed the disabled-level button `onClick` safety return. The button is
  already disabled for unavailable levels, so the branch cannot fire through
  user interaction.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Mobile Command Input Guard Cleanup

Updated:

- `src/web/mobile/CommandInputBar.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Removed the unreachable terminal-mode branch from the shared textarea
  `handleKeyDown` callback. Terminal mode renders an `<input>` with its own
  inline Enter handler, so the shared handler only needs to express AI textarea
  behavior.
- Replaced the auto-resize effect's nullable input ref guard with the mounted
  input invariant. The component always renders either the AI textarea or
  terminal input with the shared ref before effects run.
- Removed impossible terminal input parent guards in focus/blur styling. The
  input is always rendered inside the prompt container that receives the border
  color update.
- Existing tests continue to cover controlled and uncontrolled input, AI and
  terminal submission, busy-state interrupt behavior, slash autocomplete,
  voice input, recent commands, keyboard visibility, and mobile expanded mode.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Wizard Conversation View Coverage

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/InlineWizard/WizardConversationView.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/InlineWizard/WizardConversationView.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Added rendered coverage for the thinking display state where tool executions
  are visible but there is not yet streamed thinking text, proving the UI shows
  the tool activity without the `Reasoning...` fallback copy.
- Removed unreachable scroll-container null guards. The component always renders
  the root scroll container before effects run, so the scroll registration,
  auto-scroll, and new-user-message scroll paths can rely on the mounted ref.
- Removed the unreachable generic error-description fallback. `ErrorDisplay` is
  private, receives a string, and is only rendered when `error` is truthy.

Updated:

Coverage added or clarified:

  render empty cells safely while highlighting another cell.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Session List Context Menu Invariant

Updated:

- `src/renderer/components/SessionList/SessionList.tsx`.
- `src/__tests__/renderer/components/SessionList.test.tsx`.

Coverage added or clarified:

- Made the context-menu delete invariant explicit by passing the already resolved
  `Session` into the delete handler. The previous missing-session fallback was
  unreachable through the rendered UI because `SessionContextMenu` only renders
  when `contextMenuSession` is found from the same session-store snapshot.

  session leaves the store, the Remove Agent action disappears and no delete
  confirmation is shown. A review pass strengthened the test by first asserting
  that the context menu had opened.

  the available-issues loading spinner lives inside the non-loading side of the
  outer loading ternary; the layer priority fallback depends on the compiled
  `MODAL_PRIORITIES.SYMPHONY` constant being absent; the PR-status template
  nullish fallbacks are inside `> 0` guards that already require a non-nullish
  value; and missing-session active contributions do not render a session
  navigation button, so the callback's defensive `if (session)` false arm is not
  reachable from the UI.
- Current top branch gaps remain outside this file; aggregate branch coverage
  remains below 100%, and integration/E2E plus mutation/fault-injection
  requirements still need completion or documented blockers.

  synopsis skips, empty synopsis responses, non-sentence summaries, long
  metadata truncation, and non-git terminal exits.

## Phase 6 Coverage Checkpoint: Hook Branch Cleanup Batch

Date: 2026-05-21

Scope:

- Completed full-suite branch misses for `src/renderer/hooks/session/useSessionLifecycle.ts`, `src/renderer/hooks/agent/useInterruptHandler.ts`, and `src/renderer/hooks/agent/useAgentListeners.ts`.
- Added session-lifecycle coverage for clearing a non-Claude tab name, verifying `agentSessions.setSessionName` receives `null` rather than an empty string.
- Added session-lifecycle coverage for non-`Error` trash failures, verifying the user-visible toast falls back to `Unknown error` while the session is still removed.
- Added interrupt-handler coverage for queued slash-command items after normal interrupt and force-kill fallback, verifying commands restart the target tab without appending a message-style user log.
- Simplified unreachable listener branches where local invariants already guarantee the value: post-error exit state is handled by the earlier error return, `toastData.sessionId` is always assigned from the actual session ID, and parsed group-chat session IDs provide a boolean moderator flag for group-chat IDs.

## Integration Checkpoint: Symphony and Auto Run Store Isolation

Date: 2026-05-21

Scope:

- Revalidated the post-threshold integration suite after the Symphony URL/state-queue fixes and Auto Run/RightPanel store-backed tests.
- Updated `AutoRunRightPanel` and `AutoRunSessionList` integration wrappers so direct `RightPanel`/`SessionList` renders seed the Zustand stores that production now reads from, including active session, right-panel tab, document list, settings, and sidebar filter state.
- Wrapped direct `RightPanel` renders in `LayerStackProvider` and `InlineWizardProvider` where the production component tree requires them.
- Replaced the Symphony queue release no-op callbacks with definite assignment so thresholded function coverage reflects executed behavior rather than uncallable fallback functions.
- No coverage exclusions were added or widened.

Validation:

- `npx vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/AutoRunBatchProcessing.test.tsx -t 'Image Upload Hidden'` passed.
- `npx vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/AutoRunSessionList.test.tsx -t 'dragging session between groups does not affect Auto Run'` passed.
- `npx vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/AutoRunRightPanel.test.tsx -t 'expanded modal receives same content as panel'` passed.
